input.title: Food Delivery Application
input.description: A mobile-first food delivery platform connecting customers with local restaurants and delivery drivers. Customers browse nearby restaurants, order food, pay online, and track deliveries in real time. Restaurant owners manage menus, accept orders, and monitor sales. Delivery drivers receive assignments, navigate to pickups and drop-offs, and update delivery status in real time.

user_classes.Customer: End user who browses restaurants, places food orders, makes payments, tracks deliveries, and rates their experience.
user_classes.Restaurant Owner: Business operator who manages restaurant profile, menus, pricing, availability, and processes incoming orders.
user_classes.Delivery Driver: Independent contractor who receives delivery assignments, navigates to pickup and drop-off locations, and updates delivery status.
user_classes.Admin: Platform administrator who manages users, restaurants, commissions, disputes, and monitors platform-wide analytics.

definitions.Order: A confirmed food purchase containing items from a single restaurant, with delivery address, payment, and status tracking.
definitions.ETA: Estimated Time of Arrival — the predicted time for food to be delivered, updated in real time based on preparation and driver location.
definitions.Service Area: A geographic zone defined by GPS coordinates within which a restaurant offers delivery.
definitions.Surge Pricing: Dynamic delivery fee adjustment applied during peak demand periods based on order volume and driver availability.

scope: Customer registration and login with phone number OTP verification
Location-based restaurant browsing with cuisine and rating filters
Menu display with categories, item customization, and availability status
Cart management with single-restaurant constraint and promo code support
Stripe payment processing with card, wallet, and cash-on-delivery options
Real-time order tracking with driver GPS location on a map
Restaurant dashboard for menu management and order acceptance
Driver app for delivery assignments, navigation, and status updates
Two-way rating system for customers, drivers, and restaurants
In-app customer support chat with automated FAQs
Push notifications for order status and promotions
out_of_scope: Multi-restaurant orders in a single checkout
Driver fleet management and shift scheduling
In-house payment gateway — all payments through Stripe
constraints: Flutter for cross-platform mobile (iOS and Android) for all three app variants
Node.js API server with Express handles business logic and order orchestration
PostgreSQL with PostGIS extension for geographic queries (nearby restaurants, driver location)
Redis for real-time driver location caching and order status pub/sub
WebSocket connections for live order tracking and driver location streaming
All prices are in a single currency (USD) for the initial release
Maximum delivery radius per restaurant is 15 km

operating_environment.frontend: Three Flutter applications: Customer app, Restaurant Owner app, and Driver app. Deployed to iOS App Store and Google Play.
operating_environment.backend: Node.js/Express REST API deployed on AWS ECS. WebSocket server for real-time features. Redis for caching and pub/sub.
operating_environment.database: PostgreSQL with PostGIS on AWS RDS. Redis for driver location cache and session management.
operating_environment.infrastructure: AWS S3 for media storage, CloudFront CDN, Firebase Cloud Messaging for push notifications.

FR-01.title: Customer Registration and Login
requirements: Customers register with phone number and receive a 6-digit OTP via SMS for verification
After phone verification, customers complete profile with name, email (optional), and delivery address
Delivery address is captured via GPS auto-detect or manual entry with Google Places autocomplete
Customers can log in with phone number and OTP (passwordless authentication)
JWT session tokens are issued on login with 30-day refresh tokens
Customers can manage multiple saved delivery addresses with a default selection

FR-02.title: Restaurant Discovery
requirements: Home screen shows nearby restaurants within delivery range based on customer's current or saved address
Restaurants are sorted by distance, rating, estimated delivery time, or promoted status
Filters for cuisine type (Indian, Chinese, Italian, etc.), price range, dietary tags (vegan, halal, gluten-free), and minimum rating
Search by restaurant name or dish name with autocomplete suggestions
Each restaurant card shows name, cover image, cuisine tags, average rating, delivery fee, estimated delivery time, and an open/closed badge
Closed restaurants are visible but grayed out with next opening time displayed

FR-03.title: Menu and Item Customization
requirements: Restaurant menu displays items grouped by category (Starters, Mains, Desserts, Drinks)
Each menu item shows name, description, price, image, dietary tags, and availability status
Items support customization options: size (small/medium/large), add-ons (extra cheese, toppings), and special instructions text field
Add-on prices are added to the base item price
Unavailable items are visible but cannot be added to cart
Menu items can be marked as 'bestseller' or 'new' by the restaurant owner

FR-04.title: Cart and Checkout
requirements: Cart is restricted to items from a single restaurant — adding from another prompts to clear the current cart
Cart displays each item with customizations, quantity, unit price, and line total
Users can apply a promo code for percentage or fixed-amount discounts with validation
Cart summary shows item subtotal, delivery fee, service fee, discount (if any), taxes, and grand total
Delivery fee is calculated based on distance and adjusted for surge pricing during peak hours
Minimum order amount is enforced per restaurant — orders below the minimum show a shortfall message

FR-05.title: Payment Processing
requirements: Customers can pay with saved credit/debit card via Stripe, digital wallets (Apple Pay, Google Pay), or cash on delivery
New cards are tokenized and saved for future use with Stripe Customer API
Stripe Payment Intents API handles the payment with 3D Secure support
On successful payment, the order is created and the restaurant receives a notification
On payment failure, the customer sees the error and can retry or choose a different method
Refunds are processed back to the original payment method through Stripe Refunds API

FR-06.title: Real-time Order Tracking
requirements: After order placement, the customer sees a live status screen showing the order lifecycle
Order statuses: placed, accepted by restaurant, preparing, ready for pickup, driver assigned, picked up, on the way, delivered
Once a driver is assigned, a map view shows the driver's live GPS location updated every 5 seconds
ETA updates dynamically based on driver location and traffic conditions
Customer can contact the driver via in-app call or chat during active delivery
Delivery proof: driver takes a photo at drop-off, visible to the customer

FR-07.title: Restaurant Owner Dashboard
requirements: Restaurant owners manage their profile: name, description, cover image, cuisine tags, operating hours, and delivery radius
Menu management: CRUD for categories and items, set availability, mark bestsellers, update prices, upload item images
Incoming orders panel: new orders appear with an alert sound, showing items, total, and customer notes
Owners accept or reject orders within 5 minutes — unresponded orders auto-cancel with customer notification
Owners update order status: accepted → preparing → ready for pickup
Sales analytics: daily/weekly/monthly revenue, order count, average order value, top-selling items

FR-08.title: Delivery Driver App
requirements: Drivers toggle online/offline availability status
Online drivers receive delivery assignment offers showing restaurant name, pickup address, customer area, estimated distance, and earnings
Drivers accept or decline assignments within 30 seconds — declined assignments are offered to the next available driver
Active delivery screen shows pickup navigation (turn-by-turn), then switches to drop-off navigation after pickup confirmation
Drivers update status: heading to restaurant → arrived at restaurant → picked up → heading to customer → delivered
Driver location is streamed to the server every 5 seconds via WebSocket while on an active delivery
Delivery completion requires a drop-off photo uploaded from the driver's camera

FR-09.title: Ratings and Reviews
requirements: After delivery, customers rate the order (1-5 stars for food quality), the restaurant, and the driver separately
Customers can add an optional text review for the restaurant
Driver rating is based on delivery speed, professionalism, and food condition
Ratings below 3 stars prompt the customer to select a reason (cold food, missing items, late delivery, rude driver)
Restaurant and driver average ratings are displayed on their profiles
Drivers with an average rating below 4.0 over the last 50 deliveries receive a performance warning

FR-10.title: Order History and Reorder
requirements: Customers can view past orders sorted by date with status, restaurant name, total, and item summary
Each past order has a detail page showing items, customizations, prices, delivery address, and payment method
Customers can reorder from any past order — items are added to cart with the same customizations if still available
Unavailable items in a reorder are flagged and excluded from the cart
Order history supports filtering by date range and restaurant

FR-11.title: Favorites and Saved Restaurants
requirements: Customers can save restaurants to a favorites list by tapping a heart icon
Favorites page displays saved restaurants with the same card format as the home screen
Customers can save individual menu items to a 'My Favorites' collection
Saved items can be added to cart directly from the favorites page
Favorites sync across devices via the user's account

FR-12.title: Customer Support
requirements: In-app support chat allows customers to report issues with active or recent orders
Common issues have pre-built quick-response options: missing item, wrong order, late delivery, payment issue
Support agents can issue partial or full refunds from the chat interface
Automated FAQ section provides answers for common questions before connecting to an agent
Support ticket history is visible to the customer from their account

NFR-01.title: Performance
requirements: Nearby restaurant listing must return within 2 seconds for a 15km radius
Driver location updates must be received by the customer within 1 second
Order placement to restaurant notification must complete within 3 seconds

NFR-02.title: Scalability
requirements: The platform must support 50,000 concurrent active orders during peak hours
Driver location streaming must handle 10,000 concurrent WebSocket connections

NFR-03.title: Reliability
requirements: Payment and order operations must be atomic — no orphan charges or unconfirmed orders
Driver assignment continues functioning if individual drivers go offline mid-delivery
Order data must be backed up daily with point-in-time recovery

system_attributes.reliability: Payment and order creation are wrapped in atomic transactions. Failed deliveries trigger automatic reassignment to another driver.
system_attributes.scalability: Driver location uses Redis pub/sub for horizontal scaling. Restaurant queries use PostGIS spatial indexes for fast geo-filtering.
system_attributes.security: Payment data is handled by Stripe only. Phone OTP verification prevents fake accounts. Driver identity is verified during onboarding.
system_attributes.maintainability: Three separate Flutter codebases share common packages. API versioning ensures backward compatibility for app updates.

FR-01.title: Build Phone OTP Registration and Login API
FR-01.description: Create POST /api/auth/send-otp accepting a phone number, generating a 6-digit code, storing it in Redis with a 5-minute TTL, and sending via Twilio SMS API. Create POST /api/auth/verify-otp that validates the code, creates or retrieves the user, and returns JWT access (1-day) and refresh (30-day) tokens. First-time users get isProfileComplete=false. Create PATCH /api/auth/profile for completing the profile with name, email, and default address.
acceptance_criteria: OTP is sent via SMS and stored in Redis with 5-minute expiry.
Correct OTP returns JWT tokens; incorrect OTP returns 400.
Expired OTP returns 400 with option to resend.
First-time user is created with isProfileComplete=false.
Rate limiting: max 3 OTP requests per phone per 15 minutes.

FR-01.title: Build Address Management API with Geocoding
FR-01.description: Create CRUD endpoints for /api/addresses: POST (create with label, full address, lat/lng coordinates), GET (list user's addresses), PATCH (update), DELETE (remove), PATCH /:id/default (set as default). Create GET /api/addresses/autocomplete?q=query proxying Google Places Autocomplete for address suggestions. Create GET /api/addresses/reverse-geocode?lat=&lng= to resolve GPS coordinates to a formatted address.
acceptance_criteria: Addresses store lat/lng coordinates for distance calculations.
Setting a default address un-defaults the previous one.
Autocomplete returns formatted suggestions from Google Places.
Reverse geocode resolves GPS coordinates to a human-readable address.
Addresses are scoped to the authenticated user.

FR-01.title: Build Onboarding and Login Screens
FR-01.description: Implement Flutter screens: phone number input with country code picker, OTP input with 6-digit auto-advance fields and resend timer, profile completion form (name, email, address with GPS auto-detect button and Google Places search). Implement the address management screen with saved addresses list, add/edit form with map pin selector, and swipe-to-delete. Store tokens in Flutter secure storage.
acceptance_criteria: Phone input validates format based on country code.
OTP screen auto-advances focus and shows countdown for resend.
GPS auto-detect button captures location and displays the resolved address.
Address search uses Google Places autocomplete with debounced input.
Tokens are stored securely and auto-refresh on 401 responses.

FR-01.title: Create Users and Addresses Schema
FR-01.description: Define PostgreSQL tables: users (id UUID, phone VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE, name, email, avatarUrl, isProfileComplete BOOLEAN, role ENUM(customer/driver/restaurant_owner/admin), createdAt, updatedAt). addresses (id UUID, userId FK, label VARCHAR, formattedAddress TEXT, location GEOGRAPHY(POINT, 4326) using PostGIS, isDefault BOOLEAN, createdAt). Create indexes on users.phone and spatial index on addresses.location.
acceptance_criteria: Users table has unique constraint on phone number.
Addresses use PostGIS GEOGRAPHY type for accurate distance calculations.
Spatial index on addresses.location enables fast proximity queries.
Migration runs on fresh PostgreSQL with PostGIS extension enabled.

FR-01.title: Configure Twilio SMS Integration
FR-01.description: Set up Twilio account for OTP delivery. Configure a dedicated phone number for sending SMS. Store Twilio credentials (Account SID, Auth Token, phone number) in environment variables. Implement SMS delivery with error handling and fallback logging. Configure test credentials for development environment to avoid sending real SMS.
acceptance_criteria: SMS is delivered via Twilio to valid phone numbers.
Twilio credentials are loaded from environment variables.
Failed SMS sends are logged with Twilio error details.
Development environment uses Twilio test credentials without real sends.
SMS delivery completes within 3 seconds.

FR-01.title: Write Authentication and Address Tests
FR-01.description: Test OTP send and verify flow, expired OTP rejection, rate limiting enforcement, profile completion, JWT token refresh, address CRUD operations, GPS reverse geocoding, default address toggle, and Google Places autocomplete proxying. Use a mock Twilio provider in tests.
acceptance_criteria: OTP lifecycle tests cover send, verify, expiry, and resend.
Rate limiting test confirms blocking after 3 rapid OTP requests.
Address tests verify CRUD, default toggle, and PostGIS storage.
Twilio mock captures sent SMS for assertion without real delivery.

FR-02.title: Build Nearby Restaurants API with PostGIS
FR-02.description: Create GET /api/restaurants?lat=&lng=&radius=15&cuisine=&priceRange=&minRating=&dietary=&sort=distance|rating|delivery_time|promoted&page=&limit=20. Use PostGIS ST_DWithin to find restaurants within delivery radius of the customer's location. Join with restaurant operating hours to compute isOpen flag and nextOpeningTime. Calculate delivery ETA using distance/average speed. Support full-text search on restaurant name and menu item names via PostgreSQL tsvector.
acceptance_criteria: Only restaurants with delivery range covering the customer's location are returned.
Closed restaurants appear with isOpen=false and nextOpeningTime.
All filters (cuisine, price, rating, dietary) can be combined.
Search matches both restaurant names and dish names within menus.
Delivery ETA is calculated based on distance from restaurant to customer.
Query with PostGIS returns within 500ms for 5000 restaurants in the area.

FR-02.title: Build Restaurant Discovery UI
FR-02.description: Implement the Flutter home screen: location bar at top showing current address (tappable to change), horizontal cuisine filter chips (scrollable), restaurant cards in a vertical list with lazy loading. Each card shows cover image, name, cuisine tags, star rating, delivery fee, ETA badge, and promoted/closed overlay. Pull-to-refresh and infinite scroll pagination. Search bar with autocomplete for restaurant and dish names. Filter bottom sheet with cuisine checkboxes, price range slider, dietary tag chips, and minimum rating slider.
acceptance_criteria: Location bar shows current address and opens address picker on tap.
Restaurant cards display all required info with proper layout.
Closed restaurants show a gray overlay with next opening time.
Search autocomplete triggers after 300ms debounce.
Filter bottom sheet applies all selections and refreshes the restaurant list.
Infinite scroll loads next page when nearing the bottom.

FR-02.title: Create Restaurants Schema with PostGIS
FR-02.description: Define PostgreSQL tables: restaurants (id UUID, ownerId FK, name, slug UNIQUE, description, coverImageUrl, cuisineTypes TEXT[], priceRange ENUM(1-4), location GEOGRAPHY(POINT), deliveryRadiusKm INT, avgRating DECIMAL, totalReviews INT, deliveryFeeBase DECIMAL, minOrderAmount DECIMAL, isActive BOOLEAN, createdAt). operating_hours (id, restaurantId FK, dayOfWeek INT(0-6), openTime TIME, closeTime TIME). Create spatial index on restaurants.location and GIN index on cuisineTypes. Run PostGIS extension creation in migration.
acceptance_criteria: Spatial index on restaurant.location enables fast ST_DWithin queries.
GIN index on cuisineTypes enables array-contains filtering.
Operating hours support multiple time slots per day (e.g., lunch and dinner).
Delivery radius is stored per restaurant (max 15km enforced at API level).

FR-02.title: Write Restaurant Discovery Tests
FR-02.description: Test nearby restaurant query with PostGIS (within radius, outside radius), cuisine filtering, price range filtering, dietary tag filtering, combined filters, sort by distance accuracy, open/closed status based on operating hours and current time, search by restaurant name, search by dish name, pagination consistency, and query performance with 5000 seeded restaurants.
acceptance_criteria: Geo-filtering tests verify correct inclusion/exclusion at radius boundary.
Operating hours tests cover timezone-aware open/closed determination.
Combined filter tests verify all filters work together correctly.
Performance test confirms sub-500ms response with 5000 restaurants.

FR-03.title: Build Menu API with Customization Options
FR-03.description: Create GET /api/restaurants/:id/menu returning menu items grouped by category (ordered). Each item includes: name, description, price, imageUrl, dietaryTags, isAvailable, isBestseller, isNew, and customizationGroups (array of {name, type: single_select|multi_select, required: boolean, options: [{name, additionalPrice}]}). Unavailable items are included but flagged. Restaurant owner endpoints: POST/PATCH/DELETE for categories and items, PATCH /:itemId/availability toggle.
acceptance_criteria: Menu groups items by category with correct ordering.
Customization groups support single-select (size) and multi-select (toppings).
Add-on prices are returned per option for client-side total calculation.
Unavailable items appear in the menu but with isAvailable=false.
Availability toggle updates in real time for the customer app.

FR-03.title: Build Menu Browsing UI
FR-03.description: Implement the Flutter restaurant detail and menu screen: restaurant header with cover image, name, rating, delivery info, and operating hours. Scrollable category tabs that stick to the top when scrolled. Menu items per category with image, name, description, price, dietary badges, and availability overlay. Tapping an item opens a customization bottom sheet with size selector, add-on checkboxes with prices, special instructions text field, quantity stepper, and 'Add to Cart' button showing the computed total.
acceptance_criteria: Category tabs scroll horizontally and highlight the current section.
Unavailable items show a 'Currently Unavailable' overlay and disabled add button.
Customization sheet computes and displays the total price dynamically.
Required customization groups prevent adding to cart until selected.
Special instructions text field is optional with character limit.

FR-03.title: Create Menu Schema
FR-03.description: Define PostgreSQL tables: menu_categories (id, restaurantId FK, name, displayOrder INT). menu_items (id, categoryId FK, name, description, price DECIMAL, imageUrl, dietaryTags TEXT[], isAvailable BOOLEAN, isBestseller BOOLEAN, isNew BOOLEAN, displayOrder INT). customization_groups (id, menuItemId FK, name, type ENUM(single_select/multi_select), isRequired BOOLEAN, displayOrder). customization_options (id, groupId FK, name, additionalPrice DECIMAL, displayOrder). Add indexes for category lookups and item ordering.
acceptance_criteria: Items are ordered by displayOrder within their category.
Customization groups and options maintain correct ordering.
Cascade delete removes options when a group is deleted.
Dietary tags array supports GIN index for tag-based queries.

FR-03.title: Write Menu and Customization Tests
FR-03.description: Test menu retrieval with correct category grouping and ordering, item availability filtering, customization group and option structure, price calculation with add-ons, restaurant owner CRUD for categories and items, availability toggle, bestseller and new badges, and dietary tag filtering.
acceptance_criteria: Menu structure tests verify correct category grouping and item order.
Customization tests verify required/optional enforcement.
Price calculation tests verify base + add-on totals.
Owner CRUD tests verify create, update, delete, and reorder.

FR-04.title: Build Cart API with Promo Codes
FR-04.description: Create cart endpoints: POST /api/cart/items (add with menuItemId, customizations JSON, quantity — validate restaurant matches current cart or prompt clear), PATCH /api/cart/items/:id (update quantity), DELETE /api/cart/items/:id, GET /api/cart (return items with computed totals). Create POST /api/cart/apply-promo (validate code, compute discount). Cart totals: subtotal, deliveryFee (distance-based with surge multiplier from Redis), serviceFee (percentage), discount, tax, grandTotal. Enforce restaurant minimum order amount.
acceptance_criteria: Adding items from a different restaurant returns 409 with a clear/switch prompt.
Cart total calculates subtotal including all customization add-ons.
Delivery fee computes based on distance using PostGIS and applies surge if active.
Valid promo code applies discount (percentage or fixed) capped at subtotal.
Invalid or expired promo codes return 400 with specific error.
Orders below minimum amount return 400 with the shortfall amount.

FR-04.title: Build Cart and Checkout UI
FR-04.description: Implement the Flutter cart screen: item list with customization summary, quantity stepper, line totals, and swipe-to-delete. Promo code input field with 'Apply' button and success/error feedback. Price breakdown section: subtotal, delivery fee (with surge badge if active), service fee, discount, tax, and grand total. 'Proceed to Checkout' button disabled if below minimum order with shortfall message. Checkout screen: delivery address selector, estimated delivery time, payment method picker, order notes text field, and 'Place Order' button.
acceptance_criteria: Cart items show customization details and correct per-item totals.
Quantity changes update the total in real time.
Promo code shows green success badge or red error message.
Surge pricing badge appears on delivery fee when active.
Checkout validates address selection and payment method before enabling 'Place Order'.

FR-04.title: Implement Surge Pricing Engine
FR-04.description: Build a background service that calculates surge multipliers per service area based on: active order count, available driver count, and time of day. Update surge multipliers in Redis every 60 seconds. Expose GET /api/delivery/surge?lat=&lng= returning the current multiplier and delivery fee estimate for the customer's location. Surge multipliers range from 1.0x (no surge) to 2.5x. Display the surge level and estimated fee before checkout.
acceptance_criteria: Surge multiplier updates in Redis every 60 seconds.
High demand areas (many orders, few drivers) get higher multipliers.
Multiplier is capped at 2.5x maximum.
API returns current surge level and estimated delivery fee.
Customer sees surge notification before placing the order.

FR-04.title: Write Cart and Pricing Tests
FR-04.description: Test cart operations (add, update, remove, clear), single-restaurant constraint enforcement, promo code validation (valid, expired, usage limit, minimum order), delivery fee calculation with distance, surge pricing application, service fee and tax calculation, minimum order enforcement, and total computation accuracy across all fee components.
acceptance_criteria: Single-restaurant constraint tests verify rejection and clear prompt.
Promo code tests cover all validation rules.
Pricing tests verify correct total computation with all components.
Surge tests verify multiplier application on delivery fee.

FR-05.title: Build Payment Processing API
FR-05.description: Create POST /api/orders/place that: validates cart, locks item prices, creates a Stripe PaymentIntent (for card/wallet) or marks as COD, creates the order record with status 'placed'. For card payments, return the client secret for Flutter Stripe SDK confirmation. Handle Stripe webhook payment_intent.succeeded to confirm the order and notify the restaurant. Handle payment_intent.payment_failed to cancel the order and release the items. Create POST /api/payments/saved-cards to list saved cards and DELETE to remove.
acceptance_criteria: Order is created with itemized snapshot and total breakdown.
Card payment creates a Stripe PaymentIntent with accurate amount.
COD orders are created without a PaymentIntent and go directly to restaurant.
Successful webhook confirms order and notifies restaurant via push.
Failed payment cancels the order with a clear error to the customer.
Saved cards are tokenized via Stripe — no raw card data stored.

FR-05.title: Build Payment UI with Stripe Flutter SDK
FR-05.description: Implement the Flutter payment screens: saved cards list with card brand icon, last 4 digits, and default badge. Add new card form using Stripe's CardField widget. Apple Pay and Google Pay buttons using Stripe's native support. COD option with a cash icon. On 'Place Order': show loading overlay, call the order API, confirm payment with Stripe if card/wallet, handle 3D Secure redirects, and navigate to order tracking on success.
acceptance_criteria: Saved cards show brand, last 4, and expiry with default selection.
Stripe CardField validates input before enabling the pay button.
Apple Pay is available on iOS and Google Pay on Android.
3D Secure authentication flow completes within the app.
Payment failure shows specific error with retry and method-change options.

FR-05.title: Configure Stripe Payment Integration
FR-05.description: Set up Stripe account with API keys for server-side and publishable key for Flutter. Configure Stripe Customer objects created on user registration for saved cards. Set up webhook endpoint with signature verification for payment events. Configure Apple Pay merchant ID and Google Pay merchant configuration. Store all secrets in environment variables. Use Stripe test mode in development.
acceptance_criteria: Stripe Customer is created for each user on registration.
Webhook signature verification rejects tampered payloads.
Apple Pay and Google Pay are configured with correct merchant IDs.
Test mode is enforced in development and staging environments.

FR-05.title: Write Payment Tests
FR-05.description: Test order placement with card payment (success, failure, 3D Secure), COD order flow, saved card management (add, list, remove, set default), refund processing, webhook handling for success and failure events, webhook idempotency, and concurrent order placement race conditions.
acceptance_criteria: Card payment tests cover success, failure, and 3DS flows.
COD tests verify order creation without Stripe involvement.
Refund tests verify amount returned to original payment method.
Webhook idempotency tests confirm no duplicate order creation.

FR-06.title: Build Real-time Order Status and Driver Tracking API
FR-06.description: Create a WebSocket endpoint /ws/orders/:orderId that streams order status changes and driver location updates to the customer. Backend publishes status changes to Redis pub/sub channel per order. Driver location updates (lat/lng) are received every 5 seconds via WebSocket from the driver app and published to the order channel. Create GET /api/orders/:orderId/status as a REST fallback returning current status, driver info, and ETA. ETA is recalculated based on driver-to-customer distance and average speed.
acceptance_criteria: WebSocket sends real-time status updates to connected customers.
Driver location is updated every 5 seconds and forwarded to customer.
ETA recalculates on each location update using distance and speed.
REST fallback returns current status if WebSocket is unavailable.
Connection handles driver disconnection gracefully (show last known location).

FR-06.title: Build Order Tracking UI with Live Map
FR-06.description: Implement the Flutter order tracking screen: status timeline at the top (placed → accepted → preparing → picked up → on the way → delivered) with animated progress. Below the timeline, render a Google Maps view showing restaurant pin, customer address pin, and driver icon moving in real time. ETA display updates dynamically. Contact driver buttons (call and chat) appear after driver assignment. Delivery proof photo section appears after delivery with the driver's drop-off photo.
acceptance_criteria: Status timeline animates through stages as updates arrive.
Map shows driver icon moving smoothly between location updates.
ETA display updates in real time with each driver location update.
Call and chat buttons appear only when a driver is assigned.
Delivery photo is displayed after the order is marked delivered.

FR-06.title: Implement Driver-to-Customer Communication
FR-06.description: Create a simple in-app messaging channel between driver and customer for active orders. Messages are stored in Redis with a TTL matching the order lifecycle. Create POST /api/orders/:orderId/messages for sending and GET for retrieving messages. Implement call masking: route calls through Twilio to connect driver and customer without revealing personal phone numbers.
acceptance_criteria: Messages are scoped to the active order and expire after delivery.
Both driver and customer can send and receive messages.
Call masking connects both parties through a Twilio proxy number.
Personal phone numbers are never exposed to either party.

FR-06.title: Write Order Tracking Tests
FR-06.description: Test WebSocket connection and status streaming, driver location update forwarding, ETA calculation accuracy, status transition sequence validation, REST fallback accuracy, driver disconnection handling, delivery proof photo upload, and in-app messaging between driver and customer.
acceptance_criteria: WebSocket tests verify real-time status and location delivery.
ETA tests verify calculation based on distance and speed.
Disconnection tests verify graceful handling with last known location.
Messaging tests verify scoping to active orders only.

FR-07.title: Build Restaurant Owner Dashboard API
FR-07.description: Create restaurant owner endpoints: PATCH /api/owner/restaurant (update profile, hours, delivery radius), CRUD for /api/owner/menu/categories and /api/owner/menu/items (full menu management). Create GET /api/owner/orders?status=new|active|completed (list orders with filters), PATCH /api/owner/orders/:id/accept and /reject (must respond within 5 minutes), PATCH /api/owner/orders/:id/status for preparing→ready. Create GET /api/owner/analytics?period=daily|weekly|monthly (revenue, order count, avg value, top items).
acceptance_criteria: Restaurant profile updates are reflected immediately in the customer app.
New orders auto-cancel if not accepted within 5 minutes.
Order acceptance triggers driver assignment via the dispatch system.
Analytics calculate revenue, order count, and top items for the selected period.
All endpoints are scoped to the authenticated restaurant owner.

FR-07.title: Build Restaurant Owner Dashboard UI
FR-07.description: Implement the Flutter restaurant owner app: profile management screen with operating hours editor and delivery radius adjuster. Menu management with category and item CRUD, drag-and-drop reordering, image upload, and availability toggles. Orders screen with tabs for New (incoming with accept/reject and 5-minute timer), Active (preparing with mark-as-ready button), and Completed. Analytics screen with revenue chart, order count, average value, and top-selling items table.
acceptance_criteria: New orders trigger a sound alert and show a countdown timer.
Accept/reject buttons are prominent and disable after action.
Menu items can be toggled on/off with a switch control.
Analytics charts support period switching (daily/weekly/monthly).
Drag-and-drop saves the new order immediately on drop.

FR-07.title: Build Order Auto-Cancel Background Job
FR-07.description: Implement a background worker (using Bull queue with Redis) that monitors new orders. If a restaurant owner does not accept or reject within 5 minutes, the job automatically cancels the order, initiates a refund if already paid, notifies the customer with an apology message, and logs the auto-cancellation for admin analytics.
acceptance_criteria: Orders not responded to within 5 minutes are auto-cancelled.
Auto-cancelled paid orders trigger a full Stripe refund.
Customer receives a push notification about the cancellation.
Auto-cancellation events are logged for restaurant performance tracking.

FR-07.title: Write Restaurant Dashboard Tests
FR-07.description: Test restaurant profile updates, menu CRUD with ordering, order acceptance and rejection, 5-minute auto-cancel timer, status transitions (accepted→preparing→ready), analytics accuracy for revenue and top items, and access control (owner can only manage own restaurant).
acceptance_criteria: Auto-cancel tests verify 5-minute timeout and refund trigger.
Status transition tests verify correct sequence enforcement.
Analytics tests use seeded orders with known expected values.
Access control tests verify owner isolation.

FR-08.title: Build Driver Assignment and Dispatch Engine
FR-08.description: Implement the driver dispatch system: when a restaurant accepts an order and marks it preparing, find the nearest available online driver using Redis geo-sorted set of driver locations. Send a delivery offer via push notification with restaurant, customer area, distance, and estimated earnings. Create POST /api/driver/offers/:id/accept and /decline. Declined offers cascade to the next nearest driver. Accepted offers create an active delivery record. If no driver accepts within 3 rounds, notify the customer of a delay.
acceptance_criteria: Dispatch finds the nearest online driver using Redis geo commands.
Offer is sent via push notification with delivery details and earnings.
30-second acceptance window per driver before cascading.
Declined offers move to the next nearest available driver.
Accepted offer creates a delivery record and notifies the customer.
No-driver-available scenario notifies the customer of a delay.

FR-08.title: Build Driver App with Navigation
FR-08.description: Implement the Flutter driver app: online/offline toggle on the home screen, delivery offer popup with accept/decline and countdown timer, active delivery screen with two phases — Phase 1: navigate to restaurant (show map with route, 'Arrived' button, 'Picked Up' button after confirming items) and Phase 2: navigate to customer ('Arrived' button, delivery photo capture via camera, 'Delivered' button). Driver location is streamed via WebSocket every 5 seconds when on an active delivery.
acceptance_criteria: Online toggle updates availability in Redis immediately.
Delivery offer popup shows with accept/decline and 30-second timer.
Navigation integrates with Google Maps for turn-by-turn directions.
Status buttons appear sequentially: Arrived → Picked Up → Arrived → Photo → Delivered.
Camera capture is required before marking delivery as complete.
Location streaming stops when driver goes offline or completes delivery.

FR-08.title: Build Driver Location Streaming Service
FR-08.description: Implement WebSocket endpoint /ws/driver/location that receives driver GPS coordinates every 5 seconds. Store the latest location in Redis geo-sorted set for dispatch queries. When the driver is on an active delivery, publish location to the order-specific Redis pub/sub channel for customer tracking. Implement connection heartbeat to detect driver disconnection and mark as offline after 60 seconds of no signal.
acceptance_criteria: Driver location updates are stored in Redis geo set for dispatch queries.
Active delivery locations are published to the order's tracking channel.
Heartbeat timeout marks driver as offline after 60 seconds.
WebSocket handles reconnection gracefully without losing driver state.
Location updates are batched and debounced to reduce Redis write load.

FR-08.title: Write Driver and Dispatch Tests
FR-08.description: Test driver online/offline toggle, dispatch to nearest driver, offer acceptance and decline with cascade, 30-second offer timeout, location streaming and storage in Redis, WebSocket connection and disconnection handling, delivery status transitions (heading→arrived→picked up→heading→delivered), delivery photo requirement, and concurrent dispatch for multiple orders.
acceptance_criteria: Dispatch tests verify nearest-driver selection with geo queries.
Cascade tests verify decline routes to next nearest driver.
Location tests verify Redis geo set updates and pub/sub forwarding.
Concurrent dispatch tests verify no double-assignment of drivers.

FR-09.title: Build Ratings and Reviews API
FR-09.description: Create POST /api/orders/:orderId/rate accepting: foodRating (1-5), restaurantRating (1-5), driverRating (1-5), optional reviewText, and optional lowRatingReason (required if any rating <3). Validate the order is delivered and belongs to the customer. Update restaurant avgRating and driver avgRating atomically. Create GET /api/restaurants/:id/reviews (paginated). Implement driver performance check: if avgRating of last 50 deliveries drops below 4.0, create a performance warning record.
acceptance_criteria: Rating is only allowed for delivered orders owned by the customer.
Duplicate ratings for the same order return 409.
Low rating (below 3) requires a reason selection.
Restaurant and driver average ratings update atomically.
Driver performance warning triggers at <4.0 average over last 50 deliveries.

FR-09.title: Build Post-Delivery Rating UI
FR-09.description: Implement the Flutter rating screen that appears after delivery confirmation: three rating sections with star pickers for food quality, restaurant, and driver. Below-3-star selection reveals a reason picker (cold food, missing items, late, rude driver, etc.). Optional text review field. Thank-you animation on submission. Rating prompt also appears as a push notification 30 minutes after delivery if not yet rated.
acceptance_criteria: Three separate star ratings are required before submission.
Low rating reveals reason picker with predefined options.
Submission shows a thank-you animation and returns to home.
Rating reminder notification appears 30 minutes after delivery.

FR-09.title: Write Ratings Tests
FR-09.description: Test rating submission for delivered orders, rejection for non-delivered orders, duplicate prevention, low-rating reason requirement, average rating recalculation for restaurant and driver, driver performance warning trigger at <4.0 average, review pagination, and rating reminder notification scheduling.
acceptance_criteria: Rating math tests verify accurate average calculation.
Performance warning tests verify threshold detection over last 50 deliveries.
Duplicate prevention tests verify 409 on re-rating same order.

FR-10.title: Build Order History and Reorder API
FR-10.description: Create GET /api/orders/history (paginated, newest first) with filters for dateRange and restaurantId. Each order returns: orderNumber, restaurantName, restaurantImage, itemSummary (first 2 items + 'and X more'), total, status, deliveredAt. Create GET /api/orders/:id for full detail. Create POST /api/orders/:id/reorder that validates item availability, copies items with customizations to a new cart, and flags unavailable items for the UI to handle.
acceptance_criteria: Order history is scoped to the customer and paginated.
Reorder copies items and customizations to a new cart.
Unavailable items in reorder are flagged but don't block cart creation.
Price changes since the original order use current prices.
Order detail returns full item breakdown with customizations.

FR-10.title: Build Order History and Reorder UI
FR-10.description: Implement the Flutter order history screen: list of past orders with restaurant image, name, item summary, total, date, and status badge. Tappable for full detail view. 'Reorder' button on each past order. If reorder finds unavailable items, show a dialog listing removed items before proceeding to cart. Filter chips for date range and a restaurant search field.
acceptance_criteria: Order list shows restaurant branding and item summary.
Reorder button adds items to cart and navigates to cart screen.
Unavailable item dialog clearly lists what was removed.
Date and restaurant filters update the list without page reload.

FR-10.title: Write Order History Tests
FR-10.description: Test order history pagination, date and restaurant filtering, reorder with all items available, reorder with some unavailable items, reorder with price changes, and order detail retrieval accuracy.
acceptance_criteria: Pagination tests verify correct page sizes and ordering.
Reorder tests verify item copying, availability checking, and price updates.
Filter tests verify correct results for date ranges and restaurant.

FR-11.title: Build Favorites API
FR-11.description: Create POST /api/favorites/restaurants/:id and DELETE to add/remove restaurant favorites. Create POST /api/favorites/items/:id and DELETE for menu item favorites. Create GET /api/favorites/restaurants and /api/favorites/items returning paginated lists with full card data. Favorites are synced server-side to the user account for cross-device access.
acceptance_criteria: Adding a favorite is idempotent — re-adding returns 200.
Restaurant favorites return the same card data as the discovery screen.
Item favorites include restaurant context for in-cart ordering.
Favorites sync across devices via the authenticated API.

FR-11.title: Build Favorites UI
FR-11.description: Implement the Flutter favorites screens: two tabs for Restaurants and Items. Restaurant tab shows saved restaurants with the same card layout as the home screen. Items tab shows saved items with restaurant name, item image, name, price, and 'Add to Cart' button. Heart icon toggle on restaurant cards and menu items throughout the app. Empty state for each tab with prompt to explore.
acceptance_criteria: Heart icon toggles favorite status with animation.
Favorite state is consistent across all screens (home, menu, favorites).
Add to cart from favorites validates restaurant single-cart constraint.
Empty state shows an illustration with 'Start exploring' CTA.

FR-12.title: Build Customer Support Chat API
FR-12.description: Create POST /api/support/tickets (orderId, issueType enum: missing_item|wrong_order|late_delivery|payment_issue|other, description). Create GET /api/support/tickets (list customer's tickets with status). Create the messaging endpoints: POST /api/support/tickets/:id/messages and GET for chat history. Support agents use a separate admin panel to respond. Agent actions include issuing partial or full refunds via Stripe from the ticket context. Create GET /api/support/faq returning categorized FAQ entries.
acceptance_criteria: Ticket creation links to a specific order with an issue type.
Chat messages support both customer and agent participants.
Agent refund action processes through Stripe and updates the ticket.
FAQ returns categorized entries searchable by keyword.
Ticket history is scoped to the authenticated customer.

FR-12.title: Build Support Chat UI
FR-12.description: Implement the Flutter support flow: issue selector screen with predefined categories and recent order selection, chat screen with message bubbles (customer right, agent left), FAQ screen with expandable accordion sections and search. Quick-action buttons at the top of the chat for common resolutions (cancel order, request refund). Notification badge on support tab when agent replies.
acceptance_criteria: Issue selector shows recent orders and predefined issue types.
Chat loads previous messages and shows new ones in real time.
FAQ search filters entries by keyword with instant results.
Agent reply triggers a push notification with badge update.

FR-12.title: Write Customer Support Tests
FR-12.description: Test ticket creation with valid order, ticket without order (rejected for order-specific issues), chat message exchange, agent refund processing, FAQ retrieval and search, ticket status transitions (open→in_progress→resolved), and customer ticket history scope.
acceptance_criteria: Ticket tests verify order linking and issue type validation.
Refund tests verify Stripe refund processing from ticket context.
FAQ tests verify categorized retrieval and keyword search.
Status transition tests verify correct lifecycle.
